what_it_does: Defines and mounts a root React platform app that tracks an active screen, renders selected platform screens or stub placeholders, and wraps them with sidebar, top bar, and chat UI.
observations: Defines STUB_NOTES for placeholder screens: projects, dashboard, landing, files, templates, and pricing.
Uses an if/else chain to choose Gallery, CalendarScreen, BrandKit, HubSettings, or a Stub fallback.
Passes active and setActive into PlatformSidebar.
Renders TopBar with notifications set to 7 and Chats with count set to 21.
titleFor maps selected stub route IDs to display titles and falls back to the raw id.
Exposes PlatformApp on window and mounts it into the DOM element with id app.
standouts: Centralizes screen selection in a small root component.
Keeps unfinished sections represented through named stub notes.
Uses fixed notification and chat counts in the app shell.
conventions: Uses functional React components and hook state.
Uses global React and ReactDOM references rather than module imports.
Uses string route IDs for navigation state.
Uses simple conditional routing inside the component.
Uses a helper function for route-title lookup.
concerns: Screen routing is hard-coded in an if/else chain.
Notification and chat counts are hard-coded.
Fallback Stub may receive an undefined note for route IDs not present in STUB_NOTES.
notes: This file is the platform UI shell and screen switchboard for the prototype app.
questions: What active IDs can PlatformSidebar emit?
Are the hard-coded notification and chat counts placeholder values?
Should unknown active IDs intentionally render a Stub with no note?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose, root app with screen routing, matches the file's behavior.
intention_signals: It appears intended to make a navigable platform prototype where completed screens render directly and unfinished areas remain reachable through descriptive stubs.
novelty: The file combines real screen routing with explicit placeholder notes for unimplemented platform sections.
contribution: Contributes a compact root shell pattern that keeps navigation state, screen selection, placeholder destinations, top-level chrome, and browser mounting together for a fused system to preserve as an app-entry orchestration layer.
summary_for_embedding: A browser-mounted React root component for a platform prototype that routes sidebar-selected screen IDs to concrete screens or stub placeholders inside a shared shell.
intention_for_embedding: This was for presenting a navigable platform app surface while allowing incomplete sections to remain visible through descriptive stubs.

symbols.does: Renders the platform app shell and selects the active screen based on local state.
symbols.description: PlatformApp initializes the active route to gallery, chooses a screen component through conditional checks, and falls back to Stub for other route IDs. It composes PlatformSidebar, TopBar, the selected screen, and Chats into the cv-shell/cv-main layout.

symbols.does: Maps known route IDs to display titles and otherwise returns the input id.
symbols.description: titleFor provides labels for the stub-oriented route IDs projects, dashboard, landing, files, templates, and pricing. It is used when PlatformApp renders the Stub fallback so placeholder screens receive a readable title.
